sourceMovie.title: Sleepwalkers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Sleepwalkers works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gerald's Game
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sleepwalkers clicked for you, Gerald's Game (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Byzantium
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sleepwalkers clicked for you, Byzantium (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sleepwalkers?
faq.answer: Try Gerald's Game, Byzantium, Cat's Eye—each shares DNA with Sleepwalkers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sleepwalkers?
faq.answer: Sleepwalkers (1992) is best described as Horror, Fantasy, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sleepwalkers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
